name: Herman Wright
abstract: Herman Wright (1932 - 1997) was an American jazz double bassist. born in Detroit, Michigan, he began on drums as a teen before ultimately settling on upright bass.

concerts.label: Benefit Concert for the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Mitchell
about_quotes.evidence: we had a sextet for a small time a couple of years, Dave Burns, Bobby Hutcherson, Herman Wright, Candy Finch and Al and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Mitchell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: I’d walk up to there house and walk over to Herman Wright’s house, Candy Finch’s house. I’d get food in my mason jar, and I’d bring it back home to eat.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Barry Harris
about_quotes.evidence: we had a bass player named Herman Wright. All these people all came to my house
about_quotes.doc_title: Barry Harris — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: And Doug Wakins, another -- Herman Wright, the late Herman Wright. A drummer by the name of “Jacktown,” Lawrence Jackson... All these guys --
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
